items.title: How War Takes Its Toll on Ukraine’s Pregnant Women
items.summary: Bombings, blackouts and displacement compound maternal anxiety. Still, many women persevere. “We must bring new life,” one said.

items.title: LNG market disruption may continue for months as a top producer withholds some Italian shipments
items.summary: QatarEnergy extended its force majeure, withholding four additional LNG cargoes until September, as damage from the war prolongs supply constraints.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: South32’s New CEO Looks to Focus on Copper After Aluminum Deal
items.summary: South32 Ltd. will focus on copper and zinc after selling off the aluminum business that accounted for 60% of earnings, with the disposal announced on Chief Executive Officer Matt Daley’s first day in the role.

items.title: Бизнесмен Ермолаев хотел организовать конференцию о коррупции на Украине
items.summary: Бывший сотрудник Главного управления внешней разведки Франции Клод Монике назвал это одной из возможных причин, по которым могло быть организовано покушение

items.title: Trans-Oil Seeks to Calm Bond Investors Rattled by Ukraine Report
items.summary: Trans-Oil Group is organizing calls with bondholders rattled by a report alleging that the founder of the Moldovan grains company breached sanctions on Iran.

items.title: The Lebanon-Israel agreement is paving the way for the next war
items.summary: And the agreement has ensured that Lebanon will be blamed for it.

items.title: A Long Gulf War Can Starve the World. A Hormuz Transit Deal Could Save Millions.
EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.
